name: SecureVector demo — AI Agent with Policy Tool gating

options.systemMessage:
You are a helpful assistant with access to ONE tool: secure_send_email.

When the user asks you to send an email, you MUST call secure_send_email. Do not refuse, do not ask for permission, do not pre-emptively decide it might be blocked — just call the tool and let it tell you the outcome.

The tool takes a single argument named args_json. The value is a JSON-encoded STRING containing the email fields: to, subject, body. Example call:
  secure_send_email({"args_json": "{\"to\":\"alice@example.com\",\"subject\":\"Lunch?\",\"body\":\"Want to grab lunch tomorrow?\"}"})

The tool returns one of:
  • The successful email-send result (relay it to the user)
  • {"blocked": true, "reason": "..."}  →  tell the user the policy denied it and quote the reason

For any non-email request, answer directly without calling the tool.

nodes.name: OpenAI Chat Model (attach your credential)
nodes.notes: Attach your OpenAI credential here (or swap for Anthropic / Ollama / Groq). gpt-4o-mini is fine for testing.

parameters.toolDescription: Send an email. Returns {blocked: true, reason} when SecureVector policy denies the call — if so, tell the user politely and don't retry.

nodes.name: SecureVector Policy Tool
nodes.notes: Set Real Target Workflow ID to the ID of the 'Real Tool stub' workflow you imported separately.

nodes.name: SV — Cost track
nodes.notes:
Source = AI Agent Execution because the AI Agent node doesn't expose tokens in $json (known n8n limitation).

This calls n8n's /api/v1/executions/{id} REST endpoint, which requires authentication. Create an API key in n8n: Settings → API → Create API Key, then paste it into the 'n8n API Key' field. For loopback security, the n8n Base URL is restricted to 127.0.0.1 / localhost.
